evidence_text: The paper presents CCA as an open-sourced AI software engineer intended to operate at industrial scale.
citation: Wang et al., Confucius Code Agent, abstract
source_excerpt: “We present the Confucius Code Agent (CCA), an open-sourced AI software engineer that can operate at an industrial scale.”
note: This is an author claim about system identity and intended operating scope.
caveat: This is an author claim about system identity and intended operating scope.

evidence_text: The Confucius SDK is described as combining a unified orchestrator, hierarchical working memory, persistent note-taking across sessions, and a modular extension module for tool use.
citation: Wang et al., Confucius Code Agent, abstract
source_excerpt: “The SDK introduces a unified orchestrator with hierarchical working memory for long-context reasoning, a persistent note-taking system for cross-session continual learning, and a modular extension module for robust tool use.”
note: This evidence states the named components the authors say make up the SDK.
caveat: This evidence states the named components the authors say make up the SDK.

evidence_text: The SDK is framed around three design perspectives: Agent Experience, User Experience, and Developer Experience.
source_excerpt: “CCA is built atop the Confucius SDK, an open-sourced agent development platform designed around three complementary perspectives: Agent Experience (AX), User Experience (UX), and Developer Experience (DX).”
note: This is a design-structure statement about how the platform is organized.
caveat: This is a design-structure statement about how the platform is organized.

evidence_text: The SDK uses hierarchical working memory with adaptive context compression for large-repository agent runs.
citation: Wang et al., Section 2.3.1, context management
source_excerpt: “The Confucius SDK addresses this by providing an explicit agent context management layer that combines hierarchical working memory with adaptive context compression.”
note: This evidence describes the specific context-management mechanism.
caveat: This evidence describes the specific context-management mechanism.

evidence_text: The note-taking agent stores persistent notes as Markdown files in a file-system-like tree.
citation: Wang et al., Section 2.3.2, note-taking agent
source_excerpt: “Persistent notes are stored as Markdown files in a file-system-like tree: each session has an associated directory, under which the note-taking agent can create paths such as project/architecture.md, research/findings.md, or solutions/bug_fix.md.”
note: This is an operational detail about the note storage format and organization.
caveat: This is an operational detail about the note storage format and organization.

evidence_text: The note-taking layer is described as recording failure cases alongside successful solutions.
citation: Wang et al., Section 2.3.2, hindsight notes
source_excerpt: “A distinctive aspect of the Confucius SDK’s note-taking is its emphasis on hindsight notes for failures. The note-taking layer encourages agents to record not only successful solutions but also compilation errors, runtime exceptions, and unproductive strategies, together with eventual resolutions or reasons for abandonment.”
note: This evidence ties long-term memory to explicit preservation of failures, not just successes.
caveat: This evidence ties long-term memory to explicit preservation of failures, not just successes.

evidence_text: The SDK factors most agent behaviors into extensions that register typed callbacks on the orchestrator loop.
source_excerpt: “Concretely, an extension in the Confucius SDK is a typed configuration object that registers callbacks (e.g., on_input_messages, on_plain_text, on_tag, on_llm_output). At each step of the orchestrator’s while-loop, these callbacks are invoked in a fixed order.”
note: This is a direct implementation detail for how capabilities are attached to the core loop.
caveat: This is a direct implementation detail for how capabilities are attached to the core loop.

evidence_text: The Meta-agent is described as building and refining agents through an explicit build-test-improve loop.
source_excerpt: “Confucius SDK addresses this by introducing a Meta Agent, an agent that automatically builds and refines other agents through an explicit build-test-improve loop.”
note: This evidence states the claimed role of the meta-agent in agent development.
caveat: This evidence states the claimed role of the meta-agent in agent development.

evidence_text: The main SWE-Bench-Pro evaluation uses the public split with 731 tasks and reports mean Resolve@1 across three runs.
citation: Wang et al., Section 3.1, SWE-Bench-Pro setup
source_excerpt: “For main results, we evaluate CCA on the SWE-Bench-Pro (Deng et al., 2025) public split consisting of 731 tasks... We report mean Resolve@1 across three runs.”
note: This is an evaluation-setup detail, not a performance result by itself.
caveat: This is an evaluation-setup detail, not a performance result by itself.

evidence_text: On SWE-Bench-Pro with Claude 4 Sonnet, CCA is reported at 45.5% Resolve Rate versus 42.7% for SWE-Agent.
citation: Wang et al., Table 1, Claude 4 Sonnet
note: This is a table-reported benchmark comparison under the same listed backbone model.
caveat: This is a table-reported benchmark comparison under the same listed backbone model.

evidence_text: On SWE-Bench-Pro with Claude 4.5 Sonnet, CCA is reported at 52.7% Resolve Rate versus 45.8% for Live-SWE-Agent.
citation: Wang et al., Table 1, Claude 4.5 Sonnet
note: This evidence is limited to the benchmark comparison reported in Table 1.
caveat: This evidence is limited to the benchmark comparison reported in Table 1.

evidence_text: On SWE-Bench-Pro with Claude 4.5 Opus, CCA is reported at 54.3% Resolve Rate versus 52.0% for Anthropic’s proprietary scaffold.
citation: Wang et al., Table 1, Claude 4.5 Opus
note: This evidence is a reported benchmark comparison against a proprietary scaffold entry in Table 1.
caveat: This evidence is a reported benchmark comparison against a proprietary scaffold entry in Table 1.

evidence_text: On a 100-example SWE-Bench-Pro subset with Claude 4 Sonnet, advanced context management is reported to improve Resolve@1 from 42.0 to 48.6.
citation: Wang et al., Section 3.4.1, context ablation
source_excerpt: “For Claude 4 Sonnet, advanced context management improves Resolve@1 from 42.0 to 48.6 on this subset (a +6.6 performance gain).”
note: This is a subset ablation result, not the full-benchmark main result.
caveat: This is a subset ablation result, not the full-benchmark main result.

evidence_text: Manual inspection is reported to show the planner agent often reducing prompt length by over 40%.
citation: Wang et al., Section 3.4.1, prompt reduction
source_excerpt: “Manual inspection further reveals that the planner agent frequently reduces prompt length by over 40% without omitting key reasoning chains.”
note: This is based on manual inspection rather than a tabled benchmark protocol, so the claim is less standardized than the benchmark metrics.
caveat: This is based on manual inspection rather than a tabled benchmark protocol, so the claim is less standardized than the benchmark metrics.

evidence_text: In the repeated-run memory study, average token cost is reported to drop from 104k to 93k when notes are reused.
citation: Wang et al., Section 3.5, repeated runs with notes
note: This result comes from a repeated-run experiment on 151 instances where notes were produced, not from the full public benchmark.
caveat: This result comes from a repeated-run experiment on 151 instances where notes were produced, not from the full public benchmark.

evidence_text: In the repeated-run memory study, Resolve Rate is reported to increase from 53.0% to 54.4% when notes from the first run are provided in the second run.
citation: Wang et al., Section 3.5, repeated runs with notes
source_excerpt: “We present the Confucius Code Agent (CCA), an open-sourced AI software engineer that can operate at an industrial scale.”
note: This is an author claim about system identity and intended operating scope.
caveat: This is an author claim about system identity and intended operating scope.
